title: rubus thyrsoideus subf. calcicola
intro: Rubus thyrsoideus subf. calcicola is a lesser-known species of wild blackberry, primarily found in the mountainous regions of the Western United States. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain and swelling, and supporting immune function. The health benefits of Rubus thyrsoideus subf. calcicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
